puzzles.title: Animals and colors (1)

clues.text: 🐱 The cat is yellow.
🐟 The fish is blue.
🐦 The bird is green.
🐶 The dog is not red.

puzzles.grammarNote: Use 'is' to state a fact: 'The cat is yellow.' Use 'is not' to say what is false: 'The dog is not red.' Both use the verb 'to be' with a color adjective — no extra words needed.

puzzles.title: Animals and colors (2)

clues.text: 🦉 The owl is blue.
🐻 The bear is brown.
🐸 The frog is orange.
🐴 The horse is not pink.

puzzles.grammarNote: 'Gray', 'pink', and 'brown' are all color adjectives. In English, adjectives never change form — 'The horse is gray' and 'The horses are gray' use the same word. Notice: English uses 'gray', not 'grey' (though both spellings exist).

puzzles.title: Animals and colors (3)

clues.text: 🦆 The duck is yellow.
🐍 The snake is purple.
🐱 The cat is not pink.
🐱 The cat is not black.
🐶 The dog is not pink.

puzzles.grammarNote: When two negative clues point to the same animal ('The cat is not pink' and 'The cat is not black'), you can eliminate two options at once. With enough negatives, only one color remains — and that must be the answer. This deduction technique works in English grammar too: narrowing down by ruling out.

puzzles.title: Animals and habitats (1)

clues.text: 🐟 The fish is in the water.
🐦 The bird is in the tree.
🐸 The frog is not at home.
🐸 The frog is not on the farm.
🐰 The rabbit is not on the farm.

puzzles.grammarNote: English uses different prepositions for different places: 'in' for spaces that surround you ('in the water', 'in the forest'), 'on' for surfaces ('on the farm'), and 'at' for specific destinations ('at home'). These prepositions must be memorized — there is no single rule.

puzzles.title: Animals and habitats (2)

clues.text: 🦆 The duck is in the water.
🐱 The cat is at home.
🐍 The snake is not on the farm.
🐍 The snake is not in the tree.
🐻 The bear is not in the tree.

puzzles.grammarNote: 'At home' is a fixed expression in English — we never say 'in the home' to mean where someone lives. Compare: 'The cat is at home' (lives there) vs 'The cat is in the house' (is inside right now). Both are correct, but 'at home' is far more natural.

puzzles.title: Animals and habitats (3)

clues.text: 🐷 The pig is on the farm.
🦌 The deer is in the water.
🦉 The owl is not in the tree.
🦉 The owl is not at home.
🦊 The fox is not at home.

puzzles.grammarNote: Notice the three prepositions used here: 'in' (in the tree, in the water, in the forest), 'on' (on the farm), and 'at' (at home). A good trick: 'at' is used for named places thought of as destinations or roles, while 'in' is used when you picture yourself surrounded by something.

puzzles.title: Family members and pets (1)

clues.text: 👩 Mom has a bird.
👵 Grandma has a fish.
👨 Dad does not have a cat.
👨 Dad does not have a rabbit.
👧 Sister does not have a cat.

puzzles.grammarNote: Use 'has' with he, she, or a name: 'Mom has a bird.' Always use the indefinite article 'a' before a countable pet noun: 'a cat', 'a dog', 'a fish'. To negate, use 'does not have': 'Dad does not have a cat.'

puzzles.title: Family members and pets (2)

clues.text: 👴 Grandpa has a cat.
👶 The baby has a hamster.
👩 Mom does not have a bird.
👩 Mom does not have a rabbit.
👦 Brother does not have a bird.

puzzles.grammarNote: The possessive pronoun 'her' replaces a female name, and 'his' replaces a male name. 'Mom does not have a bird' can also be said as 'Her pet is not a bird.' Both sentences mean the same thing — one uses a name, the other uses a pronoun.

puzzles.title: Family members and pets (3)

clues.text: 👵 Grandma has a fish.
👧 Her pet is a bird.
👨 Dad does not have a cat.
👨 Dad does not have a rabbit.
👶 The baby does not have a cat.

puzzles.grammarNote: 'Her pet is a bird' uses the possessive pronoun 'her' to refer to sister, who was named in the previous clue. In English, pronouns must match the gender: 'her' for females, 'his' for males. Using pronouns avoids repeating the name and sounds more natural.

puzzles.title: Family members and colors (1)

clues.text: 👩 Mom's favorite color is red.
👴 His favorite color is green.
👨 Dad's favorite color is not purple.
👨 Dad's favorite color is not blue.
👧 Her favorite color is not blue.

puzzles.grammarNote: 'His favorite color is green' uses 'his' to refer to grandpa. Notice the structure: possessive pronoun + noun + 'is' + adjective. In English, color words are always adjectives — they never change form: 'His color is green', not 'His color is greens'.

puzzles.title: Family members and toys (1)

clues.text: 👶 The baby has a doll.
👩 Mom has a kite.
👨 Dad does not have a ball.
👨 Dad does not have a puzzle.
👧 Her toy is not a puzzle.

puzzles.grammarNote: 'Her toy is not a puzzle' uses the possessive pronoun 'her' to refer to sister. The structure is: possessive pronoun + noun + 'is not' + article + noun. Compare with the verb 'have': 'She does not have a puzzle.' Both sentences express the same fact — one uses 'to be', the other uses 'to have'.
